content.problemStatement:
- In this mini-project, you’ll build the logic for an Interactive Quiz Form that gives users instant feedback after answering multiple-choice questions. You’ll use JavaScript to check answers, calculate scores, and make the quiz respond interactively.

**You’ll develop this project in three clear phases:**
- Phase 1 – Handle Form Submission: Detect when the form is submitted and prevent it from reloading the page.
- Phase 2 – Grade the Quiz: Collect user-selected answers, compare them with the correct ones, and calculate the total score.
- Phase 3 – Provide Feedback & Hints: Display which answers are correct or incorrect and add “Hint” buttons that reveal helpful clues when clicked.

By the end, you’ll have an interactive quiz that reacts dynamically to user input and provides real-time feedback.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
